lesson.title: Continuous Random Variables
lesson.body: A continuous random variable X is described by a probability density function (PDF) f(x) ≥ 0 with ∫_{-∞}^{∞} f(x)dx = 1. Probabilities are areas: P(a ≤ X ≤ b) = ∫_a^b f(x)dx. The CDF is F(x) = P(X ≤ x) = ∫_{-∞}^x f(t)dt, and f(x) = F′(x). Expected value and variance generalize: E[X] = ∫ x f(x)dx and Var(X) = E[X²] − (E[X])².

rooms.question: A random variable X has PDF f(x) = 2x for 0 ≤ x ≤ 1. Verify this is a valid PDF.
answers: Yes — f(x) \geq 0 and the integral equals 1.
Yes — any quadratic function is a valid PDF.
rooms.explanation: ∫₀¹ 2x dx = [x²]₀¹ = 1, and f(x) = 2x ≥ 0 on [0,1]. Both conditions for a valid PDF are satisfied.

rooms.question: For X with PDF f(x) = 2x on [0,1], find P(0.5 ≤ X ≤ 1).
For X with PDF f(x) = 2x on [0,1], what is E[X]?
For X with PDF f(x) = 2x on [0,1], what is the CDF F(x) for 0 ≤ x ≤ 1?
rooms.explanation: F(x) = ∫₀ˣ 2t dt = [t²]₀ˣ = x². Notice that F′(x) = 2x = f(x), confirming the fundamental relationship.

rooms.question: Order these steps to compute Var(X) for a continuous random variable with known PDF f(x):
rooms.explanation: Start with the PDF, then compute E[X] and E[X²] separately as integrals, and finally apply the computational formula for variance.

boss.name: The Continuous Specter
boss.taunt: You cannot pin me down. I exist at no single point — only as an integral of dread.

questions.question: X has PDF f(x) = c·e^{−x} for x ≥ 0. What value of c makes this a valid PDF?
X ~ Uniform(0, b). What value of b makes E[X] = 3?
questions.explanation: For X ~ Uniform(0,b), E[X] = b/2. Setting b/2 = 3 gives b = 6.

questions.question: X has PDF f(x) = 2x on [0,1]. Compute Var(X).
